title: cardamine bellidifolia subsp. alpina
intro: Cardamine bellidifolia subsp. alpina, also known as alpine bittercress, is a subspecies of the cardamine bellidifolia plant, a member of the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to alpine regions in Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of alpine bittercress include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Alpine bittercress can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be used in cooking or as a food ingredient, although it is often used in small quantities due to its strong flavor and potential bitterness.
